originalTitle: 'The gravest crime against humanity': What does the UN vote on slavery mean?
title: A Big Talk About Fairness and History!
content:
Hello everyone! I'm Zola, your friendly news reporter from right here in sunny Africa! Today, we're going to talk about something very important that happened a long, long time ago, and why people are talking about it now to make our world a fairer place.

What is Slavery?

First, let's learn about a word called 'slavery.' A long, long time ago, some people were forced to work for others. They didn't get to choose their jobs, they didn't get paid, and they couldn't even go home to their families when they wanted to. They were not free. It was a very, very sad and unfair time for many people. Imagine not being able to play with your friends or choose what you eat! That's how it felt, but much worse. Did you know? Being free means you get to make your own choices, like what games to play or what to learn in school. Freedom is a super important gift that everyone should have!

Where Did This Happen?

Many, many years ago, bad people came to Africa. They took many strong men, women, and children away from their homes and families. They forced them onto big ships and sailed far, far across a huge ocean. These ships went to places like the Caribbean islands and other faraway countries. Did you know? Africa is a super big continent with many different countries, languages, and beautiful cultures! It’s one of the biggest places on Earth. Did you know? The Caribbean is a group of beautiful islands in the sea, with lovely beaches and sunny weather. But a long time ago, people from Africa were forced to work there.

Who Benefited From This?

When the people from Africa arrived in these new lands, they were forced to work very, very hard. They worked on farms growing sugar, cotton, and tobacco. They built big buildings and roads. All this hard work made the people who owned them very rich. These rich people were mostly from faraway countries. They made lots of money and built strong economies because they didn't have to pay the enslaved people for their work.

What Are African and Caribbean Countries Asking For Now?

Now, many years later, the children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren of those brave people who were enslaved are still living in Africa and the Caribbean. They remember the sad history. They want the countries that got rich from slavery to help make things fair now. This is called 'compensation.' Did you know? Compensation means making things right after something bad has happened. It's like if you accidentally broke your friend's toy, you would try to fix it or get them a new one. It's about saying 'sorry' not just with words, but with actions and help. These countries are asking for help to build better schools, hospitals, and homes. They want help to make sure everyone has good jobs and a bright future. They believe it's fair because their ancestors suffered so much, and other countries got rich from that suffering.

What is the UN?

There's a very big club called the United Nations, or UN for short. It's like a special meeting place where leaders from almost all the countries in the world come together. They talk about big problems, like how to keep peace and help people around the globe. Did you know? The UN helps countries work together like a big team to solve problems and make our world a better place for everyone!

What Did the UN Say About Slavery?

Recently, the countries in the UN had a big talk about slavery. They all agreed that slavery was a terrible, terrible thing. They called it 'the gravest crime against humanity.' This means it was one of the worst things that could ever happen to people. It caused so much pain and suffering that lasted for hundreds of years. Did you know? A 'crime against humanity' means something that hurts many, many people deeply and goes against what is right and good for all human beings. It's a very serious label.

Why is This Not Easy?

It's not easy to decide how to give compensation. It happened such a long time ago! Many of the people who were directly involved are not alive anymore. So, figuring out who should pay and how much to pay can be tricky. But even though it's hard, many people believe it's very important to talk about it and try to find a way to make things fairer.

Why is it Important to Talk About This?

Talking about slavery and its history is very important for a few big reasons. First, it helps us remember what happened so we never let such a terrible thing happen again. Second, it helps us understand why some communities might still be struggling today because of what happened to their ancestors. And third, it reminds us that everyone, no matter where they come from or what they look like, deserves to be treated with kindness, respect, and fairness. Did you know? Learning about history helps us learn from the past, so we can build a better and happier future for everyone! It teaches us to be kind and fair to all our friends, neighbors, and people all over the world. So, while it's a big and sometimes difficult conversation, it's all about making sure that the world becomes a truly fair and kind place for every single person. I'm Zola, your news reporter, wishing you a day full of learning and kindness!
summary: African and Caribbean countries are asking for help, called compensation, from countries that got rich from slavery long ago, because it was a very bad time when people were forced to work and were not free.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us about fairness, remembering sad history so it doesn't happen again, and making sure everyone in the world is treated kindly and equally today and in the future.

originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Our Pictures, Our Rules! Being Safe Online in Africa
content:
Welcome, Super Kids!

Hello, amazing friends! I'm your special news reporter, bringing you important stories from our wonderful continent, Africa. Today, we have a very important story. It's about being kind and safe when we use computers or phones. It's a story that teaches us about respect. It happened in two beautiful countries, Kenya and Ghana. These are places with lots of sunshine and friendly people, just like where you might live!

A Story from Kenya and Ghana

Imagine you are walking outside, playing or just going about your day. Someone comes up to you and starts talking. They might seem friendly. But then, they start filming you with a camera or phone, without asking you first! This is what happened to some grown-up women in Kenya and Ghana. A man, who was from far away, from a country called Russia, was doing this. He would film them in secret. This means they didn't know he was filming, or they didn't say it was okay. This made the women feel very surprised and uncomfortable. It's never okay to film someone without their permission.

What Does "Secretly Filmed" Mean?

When we say "secretly filmed," it means someone uses a camera to record you, but they don't tell you. Or, they don't ask if it's okay. Think about it like this: if your friend takes your toy without asking, how would you feel? Not very happy, right? It's the same with pictures and videos of people. Our bodies and our faces are part of us. We should always be asked before someone takes our picture or video. It's about respecting each other's personal space and choices. Everyone has the right to say "yes" or "no" to being filmed. **Did you know?** There are over 50 countries in Africa! Kenya and Ghana are two of them, known for their beautiful cultures, vibrant music, and warm, welcoming people.

What is "Consent"? Asking Permission!

This big word, "consent," just means asking for permission. It means saying "yes" or "no" to something. For example, if you want to play with your friend's ball, you ask, "Can I play with your ball?" If they say "yes," that's consent! If they say "no," then you don't play with it. It's very simple. When someone wants to film you, they should always ask for your consent first. "Is it okay if I take your picture?" That's how it should be. The women in this story were not asked. They didn't give their "yes." This is why what happened was wrong.

Then What Happened Online?

After the man secretly filmed these women, he did something even worse. He put their videos on the internet! The internet is a huge place where people share lots of things. Sometimes it's good, like sharing funny animal videos or learning new things. But sometimes, people use the internet to be mean. This man put the videos online and then, other people said unkind things about the women. They made fun of them. This is called "ridiculing" and "abusing online." It means being very, very mean with words and hurtful pictures on the internet. It made the women feel very bad.

Why Is This Not Okay? Our Feelings Matter!

Imagine someone saying mean things about you on the playground. How would that make you feel? Sad? Angry? Unhappy? That's how these women felt. It's not fair. Everyone deserves to be treated with kindness and respect. Putting someone's video online without asking is wrong. Saying mean things about them online is also very wrong. It can hurt people's feelings a lot. It can make them feel sad and scared. We all have feelings, and we need to protect each other's feelings. Being kind is always the best way to be. **Did you know?** The internet connects millions of computers all over the world, like a giant spiderweb! That's why it's so important to be careful and kind when you use it, because your words can reach many people.

Our Privacy is Important

Another big word is "privacy." Privacy means having some things that are just for you. It's like having your own secret diary that no one else reads without your permission. Or your own bedroom that is your private space. Pictures and videos of us are part of our privacy. We should get to decide who sees them and where they are shared. No one should take away our privacy without asking. These women's privacy was taken away when their videos were shared without their consent. It’s like someone opened your diary and showed it to everyone. That wouldn’t feel right, would it?

Being a Good Online Friend

The internet can be a wonderful place for learning and fun. But just like in real life, we need to be good friends and good people online. This means: * Always be kind with your words. Think before you type or share. * Never share pictures or videos of anyone without asking them first. Always wait for their clear "yes." * If you see something mean online, tell a grown-up right away. Don't be quiet! Your grown-ups can help make things right. * If someone asks you for a picture, and you don't feel good about it, you can always say "no." It's your body, your rules! You don't have to explain why. **Did you know?** Many schools in Africa are now using computers and tablets to help kids learn amazing new things, from science to art! Learning about online safety is a very important part of using these new tools.

What Can You Do to Be Safe?

Even though this happened to grown-ups, it's a very important lesson for everyone, especially for young super kids like you. Knowing these rules helps keep you and your friends safe. 1. **Always Ask:** If you want to take a picture or video of a friend, always ask them first, "Is it okay if I take your picture?" Wait for their "yes." If they say "no," then respect their choice. 2. **Protect Yourself:** If someone tries to take your picture or video without asking, you can say "no" very clearly and loudly. You can walk away quickly. If you feel unsafe, move to a trusted grown-up. 3. **Tell a Grown-Up:** If anything like this ever happens to you, or if someone is mean to you online, always, always tell a grown-up you trust. It could be your mom, dad, teacher, aunt, or uncle. They are there to help you and keep you safe. They can take action to protect you. 4. **Be an Upstander:** If you see someone being mean to another person online, you can tell a grown-up about it. Being an upstander means standing up for what is right, even if it's not happening to you. You can help someone else stay safe.

Let's All Be Kind!

This story from Kenya and Ghana reminds us of a very important lesson: everyone deserves to be treated with kindness and respect, both in real life and online. Our pictures and videos are ours, and we decide who sees them. Let's make our part of the internet a happy and safe place for everyone. Remember, your voice matters, and your safety is super important! Keep shining bright, super kids!
summary: A man secretly filmed women in Kenya and Ghana, then put their videos online and people were mean to them, teaching us why it's super important to always ask permission before taking pictures or videos, and to be kind and safe when we use the internet.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone has the right to decide who sees their pictures and videos (this is called privacy!), and we should always be kind and respectful to each other, especially online, to keep everyone safe and happy from unkind words and actions.
